formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the complete formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Accountancy - Introduction to Accounting in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Accounting Equation",
    "formula": "Assets = Capital + Liabilities",
    "variables": "Assets: Economic resources owned by a business, Capital: Owner's capital + Profit/Loss, Liabilities: Amounts owed by a business",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This equation is the foundation of accounting and represents the relationship between assets, capital, and liabilities of a business"
  },
  {
    "name": "Accounting Entity",
    "formula": "Accounting entity is a separate entity for accounting purposes",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept is based on the principle that a business is treated as a separate entity for accounting purposes, distinct from its owners and other businesses"
  },
  {
    "name": "Going Concern",
    "formula": "The business will continue to operate for the foreseeable future",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept assumes that a business will continue to operate for the foreseeable future, and financial statements are prepared on this basis"
  },
  {
    "name": "Historical Cost",
    "formula": "Assets and liabilities are recorded at their historical cost",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept involves recording assets and liabilities at their original cost, rather than their current value"
  },
  {
    "name": "Matching Concept",
    "formula": "Expenses are matched with revenues in the same period",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept involves matching expenses with revenues in the same period, to provide a true and fair view of a business's financial performance"
  },
  {
    "name": "Materiality",
    "formula": "Omissions or misstatements of accounting information are material if they could influence the decisions of users",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept involves considering whether omissions or misstatements of accounting information could influence the decisions of users, and if so, they are considered material"
  },
  {
    "name": "Consistency",
    "formula": "Accounting policies and procedures are applied consistently",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept involves applying accounting policies and procedures consistently, to provide a true and fair view of a business's financial performance"
  },
  {
    "name": "Accrual Concept",
    "formula": "Expenses are recognized when incurred, and revenues are recognized when earned",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept involves recognizing expenses when incurred, and revenues when earned, rather than when cash is received or paid"
  },
  {
    "name": "Periodicity",
    "formula": "Financial statements are prepared at regular intervals",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "This concept involves preparing financial statements at regular intervals, to provide a true and fair view of a business's financial performance"
  }
]
```

Note: The formulas and laws included in this list are based on the standard accounting principles and concepts, and are relevant to the Introduction to Accounting chapter of the CBSE Class 11 Accountancy syllabus.
